meta.purpose: Config + data for the FBLA role-play generator. Three parts: (A) situation maker, (B) audio/script grader, (C) question generator. FBLA scores the role-play PERFORMANCE on one shared rating sheet; only the event-specific line(s) change per event.
meta.applies_to: FBLA high-school Role Play events (the 12 hybrid test + role-play events).
sources: connect.fbla.org official guidelines (S3, not directly readable) — verified via readable mirrors below
Banking & Financial Systems: cteresource.org/media/2jamyfhq/2526-banking-and-finance-va-fbla.pdf (VA FBLA = national verbatim)
meta.scope_note: 3 of 12 events built first (Marketing, Customer Service, Banking & Financial Systems) to validate the format. The other 9 reuse the SAME shared rating sheet — only knowledge_areas and the event-specific line(s) differ.

output_contract.scenario: { event, role, company, situation, your_task, judge_role, knowledge_areas_in_play:[...], prep_minutes, perform_minutes, participants }
output_contract.grade: { toolId:'roleplay', event, results:[ {criterion, category, max, band, points, justification, fix, gradable_from} ], penalties:[...], total:{scored, of:100}, meta:{transcript, input_mode} }

how_fbla_scoring_works.shared_rating_sheet: All FBLA role-play events are scored on ONE common 7-line, 100-point rating sheet. Four bands per line: 'Not Demonstrated' (0), 'Below Expectations', 'Meets Expectations', 'Exceeds Expectations'.
how_fbla_scoring_works.the_only_event_specific_line: Line 'Demonstrates knowledge of the event knowledge areas' — the knowledge_areas differ per event and are the substance of that line.
how_fbla_scoring_works.event_variants: A few events swap content lines for custom ones (e.g., Customer Service replaces two lines with Empathy/Diplomacy and Conflict Resolution, each worth 20). Each event below therefore carries its OWN explicit rating_sheet so the grader never has to guess.

band_point_ranges.comment: Point ranges are standardized by the line's max. Use these exactly.

penalties.reason: Late arrival / failure to follow event procedures

gradable_from_legend.script: Content lines — gradable from a typed script OR an audio transcript.
gradable_from_legend.audio: Verbal delivery (voice projection, pace, fluency) and answering questions — gradable from audio.
gradable_from_legend.video: Body language and eye contact — need video for a full score; from audio alone, grade only the voice portion and flag the visual portion as not assessed.

context_banks.comment: The situation maker draws ONE value from each bank (+ temperature + recent-history exclusion) to make every scenario unique while the rating sheet stays fixed. Prefer the event's domain_flavor for on-topic settings.
company_sizes: a startup with a handful of employees
constraints: a competitor just changed the game
twists: the client is unhappy and considering leaving
leadership wants a recommendation by tomorrow
two options are on the table and you must pick one
a mistake was made that must be addressed

rating_sheet.criterion: Demonstrates understanding of the role play and defines the problem(s) to be solved
Identifies alternatives and the pro(s) and con(s) of each
Identifies a logical solution and aspects of implementation
Demonstrates knowledge and understanding of the event knowledge areas
Statements are well-organized and clearly stated
Consistently displays confidence, poised body language, and engaging eye contact
Demonstrates the ability to effectively answer questions
Demonstrates understanding of the role play and defines the problem(s) to be solved
Communicates position on the role play scenario
Identifies a logical solution and aspects of implementation
Displays empathy/diplomacy when responding to the role play scenario
Demonstrates conflict resolution and closure to the role play
Statements are well-organized and clearly stated
Consistently displays confidence, poised body language, engaging eye contact, and effective voice projection
Demonstrates the ability to effectively answer questions

knowledge_areas: Concepts and Practices (banking operations, accounting, Federal Reserve, FDIC, accounts, loans)
Basic Terminology (credit, payroll, taxation, mortgages, stocks/bonds, investments, insurance)
Government Regulation (laws, compliance, audits, records retention)
Technology Impact (financial software, databases, online services)
Institution Types (banks, credit unions, services, credit sources)

rating_sheet.criterion: Demonstrates understanding of the role play and defines the problem(s) to be solved
Identifies alternatives and the pro(s) and con(s) of each
Identifies a logical solution and aspects of implementation
Demonstrates knowledge and understanding of the event knowledge areas
Statements are well-organized and clearly stated
Consistently displays confidence, poise, eye contact, and effective voice projection
Demonstrates the ability to effectively answer questions
